name: El Capricho de Gaudi, the Abadia Cisterciense de Santa Maria y Santa Maria de los Angeles - return from San Vicente de la Barquera
name_original: El Capricho de Gaudi, la Abadía Cisterciense de Santa María y Santa María de los Ángeles – vuelta desde San Vicente de la Barquera
region: Lic-Rias Occidentales Y Duna De Oyambre
region_intro: Touring cycling in Lic-Rias Occidentales Y Duna De Oyambre offers diverse routes through a protected natural area in Cantabria, Spain. The region features a coastline with extensive sandy beaches, significant dune systems, and cliffs ranging from 10 to 50 meters in height. Inland, the landscape transitions to small hills, meadows, and streams, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Estuaries such as Tina Mayor and San Vicente are prominent, contributing to the area's rich biological diversity and scenic routes. Best touring cycling routes in Lic-Rias Occidentales Y Duna De Oyambre The most popular touring cycling route is El Capricho de Gaudi, the Abadia Cisterciense de Santa Maria y Santa Maria de los Angeles - return from San Vicente de la Barquera, a 29.4 miles (47.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route connects cultural landmarks and offers varied scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is San Vicente de la Barquera to Comillas – circular through the Oyambre Natural Park, a difficult 29.4 miles (47.3 km) path. This route provides coastal views and traverses the Oyambre Natural Park. Local touring cyclists also love the San Vicente de la Barquera – View of the Picos de Europa loop from San Vicente de la Barquera, a 15.3 miles (24.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 39 minutes. Touring cycling in Lic-Rias Occidentales Y Duna De Oyambre is defined by coastal roads, routes alongside estuaries, and paths through meadows and rolling hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Lic-Rias Occidentales Y Duna De Oyambre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lic-Rias Occidentales Y Duna De Oyambre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Lic-Rías Occidentales y Duna de Oyambre?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes available in Lic-Rías Occidentales y Duna de Oyambre, offering a variety of experiences through this protected natural area. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Lic-Rías Occidentales y Duna de Oyambre?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You'll find coastal roads with dramatic cliffs and extensive sandy beaches, routes winding alongside biologically rich estuaries like Tina Mayor and San Vicente, and paths through rolling hills and meadows further inland. The terrain includes options from flat, easy stretches to more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente de la Barquera loop from San Vicente de la Barquera&lt;/a&gt; is a gentle 6 km ride with minimal elevation gain, offering a pleasant experience through the town's surroundings.

Q: Can I find challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012252332/el-capricho-de-gaudi-the-abadia-cisterciense-de-santa-maria-y-santa-maria-de-los-angeles-return-from-san-vicente-de-la-barquera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Capricho de Gaudi, the Abadia Cisterciense de Santa Maria y Santa Maria de los Angeles - return from San Vicente de la Barquera&lt;/a&gt; cover nearly 47 km with significant elevation changes, connecting cultural landmarks and offering varied scenery.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes in Lic-Rías Occidentales y Duna de Oyambre pass by numerous natural and historical landmarks. You can enjoy views of the extensive Oyambre beach and its significant dune systems, dramatic cliffs, and the tranquil estuaries. Notable points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint and Church of Santa María de los Ángeles&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2287761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Maza&lt;/a&gt;, and scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Zapedo marsh&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017302055/san-vicente-de-la-barquera-to-comillas-circular-through-the-oyambre-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente de la Barquera to Comillas – circular through the Oyambre Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning coastal views and traverses the natural park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Lic-Rías Occidentales y Duna de Oyambre?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling from spring through autumn. The summer months offer warm weather ideal for coastal rides and enjoying the beaches. Spring and early autumn provide milder temperatures and lush landscapes, making them excellent for exploring the varied terrain without the peak summer crowds.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Picos de Europa mountains?
A: Yes, some routes provide spectacular distant views of the Picos de Europa. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1287001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente de la Barquera – View of the Picos de Europa loop from San Vicente de la Barquera&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 24.6 km trail specifically designed to showcase these impressive mountain vistas.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Lic-Rías Occidentales y Duna de Oyambre?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse scenery, which includes dramatic coastlines, tranquil estuaries, and rolling inland hills, as well as the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable cycling experience.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that pass through charming towns or villages?
A: Many routes connect the region's charming towns and villages. For instance, the route from San Vicente de la Barquera to Comillas takes you through the Oyambre Natural Park and allows you to explore Comillas, known for its unique architecture like Gaudi's 'El Capricho'.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on touring cycling routes in this region?
A: Elevation gains vary significantly depending on the route's difficulty. Easy routes, like the San Vicente de la Barquera loop, might have around 40 meters of elevation gain. More difficult routes, such as those traversing the Oyambre Natural Park, can feature over 790 meters of ascent, providing a good workout for experienced cyclists.
tips:
San Vicente de la Barquera is an impressive fishing village located in the heart of the Oyambre natural park. Located between two estuaries, San Vicente faces the sea. The landscape changes constantly with the tides and the sight of the boats perched on the sand at low tide is very particular.

In Roman times, San Vicente was already an important port. In the Middle Ages, notable buildings such as the church of Santa María de los Ángeles were built and today the remains of the wall and the castle remain. However, the most appreciated monument is the stone bridge of La Maza, with its 28 eyes, built by order of the Catholic kings in the 16th century. From this bridge you can appreciate one of the most characteristic views of San Vicente, the contrast of the snow-capped peaks of Europe and the mouth of the estuary to the Cantabrian Sea.

The Puente de la Maza (15th century), with 28 eyes and more than half a kilometer in length, allows us to cross the San Vicente estuary to the entrance of San Vicente de la Barquera, a beautiful Cantabrian town full of treasures, with an environment magnificent natural environment and an enviable artistic heritage. Its port was and still is the heart of its activity and the most important in the region. It has a really impressive medieval set.

One of the many masterpieces of the famous Gaudí, the house called "Capricho" is perhaps not as recognized as his main works of art but it is undoubtedly a house to visit for its charm and characteristic architecture that make it a unique piece in its style. .

The original Cistercian abbey is now a Trappist monastery dedicated to Santa Maria Viaceli. It was built in the neo-Gothic style (1906-1910). In 2013 it was declared an Asset of Local Interest in Cantabria. They sell the excellent Trappist cheese "Queso Trapa" in the little shop on the street.

The Oyambre estuary or La Rabia estuary is an estuary located between the municipalities of San Vicente de la Barquera, Valdáliga and Comillas. Seventeen species of birds use these fertile waters to feed and rest in winter. The mallard, common teal and great cormorant are representative of this ecosystem.

The old town of San Vicente de la Barquera is ideal for a trip back in time. Sometimes you can hear the bagpipes playing in the small streets. The old buildings are majestic and impressive. Next to the church, there is a viewpoint with a panoramic view of the sea and the mountains.
